answer:
The following works in the corpus contain explicit descriptions of feasting or hospitality (or a perverse inversion thereof). These are drawn directly from search results and confirmed passages; metaphorical or incidental mentions (e.g., poetic wine "feasts" in *Diwan-E-Ghalib*) are excluded.

**Cynewulf, *Andreas***: Describes the Mermedonians' custom of devouring foreign guests as a "foul, grim feast," with their bodies prepared as a "bloody banquet."[^1][^2]

**Unknown, *Old English Elegies* ("Soul and Body II")**: The soul rebukes the body for being "proud with your feasting, sated with wine."[^3]

**Unknown, *Pārāśara Smṛti***: Prescribes "hospitality to an *atithi* (a guest, who must be honored)" as one of six daily duties.[^4]

**Unknown, *Yājñavalkya Smṛti***: Lists "hospitality to guests" among the "great sacrifices (*mahāyajñas*)" owed to beings, ancestors, gods, Brahman, and men.[^5]

**Unknown, *Āṅgirasa Smṛti***: Permits (with caution) "a large feast afterwards" following certain rites.[^6]

**Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL III***: Multiple chapters detail Arab Bedouin hospitality as a sacred virtue extended to all strangers (regardless of faith), including sharing scant food, granting inviolable sanctuary in tents even to enemies, and viewing violations as eternal shame.[^7]

No other works in the corpus provide explicit descriptions of these topics.

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-f6e450).

[^2]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-4d1e78).

[^3]: Unknown, *Old English Elegies*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/10-soul-and-body-ii/translation/#p-00ea0f).

[^4]: Unknown, *Pārāśara Smṛti*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-40daa2).

[^5]: Unknown, *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-76e5e9).

[^6]: Unknown, *Āṅgirasa Smṛti*, [paragraph](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-f62f9f).

[^7]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL III*, [passage](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/30-des-rapports-observes-entre-les-moyens-d-existence/translation/#p-9c11e3).
